text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025

Dr Fiona
Centre for Epilepsy
Queen Square, London WC1N 3BG

Dear Dr Fiona,

Alex Green, DOB: 22-11-1991, Hospital No: Q274619 NHS No. 9817564203
Flat 3, 7 Market Row, London, SE1 9AB

Epilepsy

Medication: No regular anti-seizure medication at present.

Previous therapy: Trial of Levetiracetam in 2023 for 3 months, discontinued due to mood change; no subsequent maintenance therapy.

Lamotrigine Blood Level:

Not applicable (no current prescription).

Seizures: Patient reports “TC ×ten/yr” over the past 12 months, usually occurring in the late evening without clear precipitant. No consistent aura reported. Post-ictal confusion lasts approximately 15–30 minutes per event. No injuries described in the past year. Events witnessed on two occasions by a colleague, who described generalised stiffening followed by rhythmic jerking lasting under two minutes with prompt recovery.

History and review: I conducted an in-person review today. The patient commutes mainly by bicycle to work and keeps a simple calendar record of events, which aligns with the reported frequency “TC ×ten/yr”. Sleep pattern is variable during peak workload periods; caffeine intake is moderate. No recent intercurrent illness.

Plan discussed: The patient wishes to consider re-trial of a different medication at a later date and will keep a seizure diary. We agreed to obtain routine bloods and arrange a follow-up discussion after results are available.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: Patient reports “TC ×ten/yr” over the past 12 months

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: Patient reports “TC ×ten/yr” over the past 12 months, and elsewhere confirms the calendar record aligns with the reported frequency “TC ×ten/yr”. This indicates 10 tonic-clonic seizures per year. No other differing frequencies are provided. Therefore, the normalized label is 10 per year.
seizure_frequency_number: Patient reports “TC ×ten/yr” over the past 12 months
